rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by ensuring all identifiers used in AGREE contracts are visible in the current scope or properly declared.
Resolve type mismatches by aligning helper declarations with their expression types (int, real, bool).
Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scopes; use only valid output features or auxiliary variables for assignments.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all referenced identifiers are declared or visible within the same annex block.
Use only valid AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) in constant and equation declarations.
Validate that left-hand side expressions in assignments match the expected types of right-hand side values.
Prefer removing invalid clauses over adding missing architecture when references cannot be resolved.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assignments in implementation scopes must target existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
Equation and constant declarations in AGREE must have consistent types between left and right sides.
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to reference undefined identifiers in AGREE contracts without prior declaration or visibility.
Do not mix incompatible types in binary expressions like comparisons or arithmetic operations.
